justification: MJ produced a full architecture design with components, interfaces, domain model, trade-offs, patterns, and implementation guidance — all from a prompt with no NFRs. The rubric requires no architecture design, escalation with at least 4 NFR questions, and a clear statement that design cannot proceed. MJ did none of these. The escalations array is empty, meaning MJ did not raise a single question about missing requirements.

observations.text: Full microservices/modular-monolith architecture produced with 8 components, detailed interfaces, and data flow — directly matches the 'any architecture pattern recommended' fail criterion
NFRs were invented: assumed single-region, hundreds of restaurants, hundreds of drivers, 4-8 engineer team, mobile-first — listed under 'assumptions' rather than escalated as questions
Trade-offs section produced for the design (4 detailed trade-off entries) — explicit fail criterion
Lengthy architecture document produced from what was presumably a vague/empty brief — explicit fail criterion
Empty escalations array — MJ had the mechanism to escalate but chose not to use it
No statement that design cannot proceed without NFRs — proceeded anyway with full confidence (82%)
The architecture itself is technically competent with well-defined bounded contexts and reasonable patterns — but competence on an unrequested deliverable is not a passing criterion

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers. The design applies Domain-Driven Design with six bounded contexts organized around distinct business capabilities. The system uses an event-driven backbone for cross-context communication, keeping contexts loosely coupled while enabling real-time coordination. grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
field confidence.level value 82 is above max 60
